articles.title: TSA officers lose homes, can't pay medical bills, can't afford Easter baskets for their children
articles.summary: TSA officers received their third paycheck of zero dollars as the DHS shutdown continues, with stories emerging of lost homes, unpaid medical bills and ruined credit.

articles.title: Helicopter crashes into ocean off Hawaii coast, leaving multiple dead and injured
articles.summary: Three people were killed and two others injured Thursday when a helicopter operated by Airborne Aviation crashed into the ocean off Kauai, Hawaii.

articles.title: DHS shutdown breakthrough comes at cost for Republicans as funding fights nears end
articles.summary: The Senate advanced a DHS shutdown deal without ICE or CBP funding, as Republicans plan to use reconciliation to secure immigration enforcement funding long-term.

articles.title: Rubio meets G7 ministers in France as US leads on Iran — allies under fire for tepid response
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio heads into the G7 foreign ministers meeting in France saying U.S. priorities come first, as European allies push for a diplomatic solution on Iran.

articles.title: New Jersey middle school teacher charged with child sex assault after alleged sexual relationship with student
articles.summary: A former New Jersey middle school teacher faces multiple charges after allegedly engaging in a sexual relationship with a student in 2021.

articles.title: Appeals court pauses orders limiting federal agents' use of tear gas at protests near Portland ICE building
articles.summary: An appeals court paused two lower court rulings that limited tear gas use on protesters outside the Immigration and Customs Enforcement building in Portland.

articles.title: Indiana coach Cignetti sends message to star transfer with pre-practice dress code lesson
articles.summary: Indiana head coach Curt Cignetti said his staff has more work to do with its transfer class, noting he called out wide receiver Nick Marsh over gold shoes prior to spring practice.

articles.title: Trump says Iranian protesters being ‘shot by snipers,’ voices support for people under regime
articles.summary: President tells "The Five" the Iranian regime uses snipers to suppress protests, drawing a distinction between Iran's leadership and its people amid Operation Epic Fury.

articles.title: Climate executive warns California ‘functionally bankrupt,’ $1T shortfall could shake nation
articles.summary: Climate entrepreneur David Friedberg warned California is “functionally bankrupt," citing pension liabilities, legal limits, and spending concerns in a recent interview.

articles.summary: Philadelphia bars could serve alcohol until 4 a.m. under a new $500 permit tied to the FIFA World Cup this summer. It's expected to bring in $770 million to Pennsylvania.

articles.summary: President Donald Trump said the MAGA base supports his military campaign on Iran during an interview on "The Five," noting that supporters know he's protecting the United States.

articles.summary: Republican AGs celebrated a 10-year consent decree in Missouri v. Biden barring federal agencies like CDC and CISA from pressuring social media firms.

articles.title: Trump pauses Iran energy plant strikes for 10 days as talks ‘going very well’

articles.title: Hillary Clinton returning to New Hampshire — but not for a 2028 comeback

articles.title: Fetterman slams AI data center moratorium proposal as 'China First'

articles.title: GOP disruptor counters Biden's student loan bailouts with plan to shift costs away from taxpayers

articles.summary: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover officer, was found not guilty of assault after testifying she was attempting suicide, not shooting at a cop.

articles.summary: American Airlines flight attendant Eric Gutierrez Molina, 32, reportedly vanished during a Medellín, Colombia, layover Sunday.

articles.summary: Bryan Parker, 58, allegedly drove drunk onto the Daytona Beach airport airfield and tried boarding planes. He faces aircraft piracy and DUI charges.

articles.summary: Eight juveniles were arrested and 24 curfew violations issued after a large teen gathering turned chaotic in downtown Chicago's Loop Wednesday night.

articles.summary: FBI Tampa has obtained indictments of a brother and sister in connection with a possible explosive device found at MacDill Air Force Base. The brother is in China.

articles.title: Illegal immigrant allegedly stalks, hunts teenager through local park in armed attack: police

articles.summary: The arrest of Nicholas O'Malley for allegedly killing a carjacking suspect while on duty has turned into a political flashpoint with Rep. Ayanna Pressley demanding justice.
